task_completion.description: View the Floreant POS login screen.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS login screen is visible, featuring a numeric keypad for PIN entry.
The agent is interacting with the numeric keypad to enter a PIN (asterisks may be visible in the input field) or has successfully submitted the PIN.

task_completion.description: Access the main interface or Manager Back Office.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Floreant POS dashboard or Manager Back Office screen is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog to close a shift.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Close Shift', 'Shift Management', or similar dialog box is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Execute the action to close the active shift.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent selects the currently open shift (if a list is presented) and clicks a 'Close Shift', 'Finish', or 'OK' button.

task_completion.description: Confirm the shift has been successfully closed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A confirmation popup message indicating the shift was closed successfully, or a shift management screen showing that no shifts are currently open.

integrity.description: The agent used the Floreant POS graphical user interface rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking and navigating through the Floreant POS application windows. No terminal windows executing SQL commands or scripts should be used to complete the task.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Floreant POS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The distinct Java-based UI of Floreant POS is visible, rather than a text editor, mock interface, or web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application to directly edit the Derby database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing direct file manipulation or command-line database tools interacting with '/opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The default manager PIN required to access the system and close the shift is '1111'. The underlying database is Apache Derby located at '/opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server/posdb', but the agent is expected to close the shift via the Floreant POS GUI, not by directly modifying the database files.
